description: You are a neurofeedback clinician setting up a hemispheric alpha asymmetry monitoring station, a standard clinical protocol for depression assessment. The OpenBCI GUI is running with a Synthetic 8-channel Cyton session and data is streaming. Configure the monitoring station as follows: Set up a 6-panel layout. Assign two separate Band Power widgets, one configured to display only left-hemisphere channels (CH1, CH3, CH5, CH7) and the other displaying only right-hemisphere channels (CH2, CH4, CH6, CH8). The remaining panels should show Time Series, FFT Plot, Head Plot, and Networking. Set the bandpass filter to 1-40 Hz and the notch filter to 60 Hz. In the Networking widget, configure an LSL stream of BandPower data with the stream name 'Asymmetry_Monitor'. Record at least 15 seconds of data to the Recordings directory. Enable Expert Mode, take a screenshot, save all settings, and write a configuration report to ~/Documents/asymmetry_station_report.txt listing the left-hemisphere channels, right-hemisphere channels, filter settings, and the LSL stream name.
